currentFocus: Abandoning downstream correlation forensics — he created 5 tasks, then immediately vetoed all 5 (misclassifications, pattern co-occurrence, RPC cross-ref, SQL metrics, tool sequences). This is a hard pivot away from the correlation dead-end.
Shifting to upstream middleware instrumentation — token logging + response status + early_stop_trigger at the API boundary, not downstream pattern-matching.
frustrations: Correlation/pattern-matching tasks feel speculative and wasteful — he knows they won't give him the deterministic root cause he needs, so he's rejecting them before they waste cycles.
Manual inbox-driven forensics drowns signal in noise; he needs atomic, deterministic detection at request time, not post-hoc query analysis.
excitements: He's cracked the mental model: upstream middleware detection (token_spent + response_status + completion_marker logged per Anthropic call) beats all downstream correlation queries — this is the architecture breakthrough he's been hunting.
The instrumentation rhythm works when tasks are tiny, zero-friction utilities (timestamp loggers, perf traces, checkpoint handlers) that compound deterministically.
inferred.nextSuggestion: Ship a single `api_response_lifecycle_log` table + middleware that atomically logs token_spent + response_status + early_stop_trigger + completion_marker for every Anthropic call, then give him a 3-line query to spot truncated requests — kills correlation guesswork cold.
